items.title: Verify derivatives on simple cases
items.avoid: Trusting a long manual derivative without checking on a few numeric points.
items.prefer: Compare analytic f′(x) to central finite differences or `gradcheck` for custom ops.
items.why: Early catches prevent wrong gradients from poisoning entire training runs.
items.example: After deriving σ′, assert σ′(0) ≈ 0.25 via (σ(1e-5)-σ(-1e-5))/(2e-5).

items.title: Watch differentiability at kinks
items.avoid: Assuming smooth derivatives through ReLU, max or clamp without convention.
items.prefer: Document subgradient choice or use smooth surrogates when analyzing theory.
items.why: Optimization still works in practice but analysis and custom autograd need consistent rules.
items.example: PyTorch uses subgradient 0 for ReLU at x=0; do not expect unique classical derivative there.
